perturbation.original_text: The Parties are working cooperatively to develop and cooperate to expand the GINARES projects. The Parties agree to develop a list of target cooperation projects and common goals, and consequent agreements if required, within six (6) Months of signing this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties are working cooperatively to develop and cooperate to expand the GINARES ventures. The Parties agree to develop a list of target partnership projects and common objectives, and subsequent understandings if needed, within twelve (12) Months of signing this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'projects' is being replaced with 'ventures', 'partnership projects' with 'target cooperation projects', 'common goals' with 'common objectives', 'agreements' with 'understandings' and 'six (6) Months' with 'twelve (12) Months'. This introduces uncertainty about what the parties initially agreed upon and extends the deadline, potentially causing disputes over timelines and deliverables. The change in terminology from 'agreements' to 'understandings' weakens the enforceability of future collaborations, as understandings are less binding than formal agreements. This is contradicting with section 3 that creates obligation for the parties on the first agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Obligations Of The Parties 3.1. HPIL ET's obligations under this Agreement are to: (i) Follow up on developments regarding GINARES; (ii) Work with GINARES as appropriate. 3.2. GINARES's obligations under this Agreement are to: (i) Keep HPIL ET aware of developments regarding GINARES; (ii) Work with HPIL ET as appropriate.
perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the deadline for developing target projects from six to twelve months and weakens the enforceability of future collaborations by changing 'agreements' to 'understandings'. This contradicts section 3, which outlines specific obligations for both parties concerning GINARES, implying an agreement is already in place or expected within a shorter timeframe. The extension and weakened enforceability create a direct conflict with the existing obligations.

perturbation.original_text: All notices and other communications required or permitted hereunder shall be in writing and shall be deemed to have been duly given (a) when delivered in person, (b) five (5) business days after being sent by registered or certified mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid, (c) when dispatched by electronic facsimile transmission (with confirmation of successful transmission), or (d) one (1) business day after having been dispatched by an internationally recognized overnight courier service, in each case to the appropriate Party at the address or facsimile number specified below:
perturbation.changed_text: All alerts and other correspondence mandated or allowed hereunder shall be in writing and shall be considered to have been properly issued (a) upon personal delivery, (b) ten (10) business days after mailing via standard post, return receipt unnecessary, postage collect, (c) upon sending via digital fax (with transmission confirmation), or (d) two (2) business days post dispatch via a nationally known express courier, in each scenario, to the relevant Entity at the address or fax number indicated below:
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'notices' is being replaced with 'alerts', 'communications' with 'correspondence', 'required or permitted' with 'mandated or allowed', 'duly given' with 'properly issued', 'delivered in person' with 'upon personal delivery', 'five (5) business days' with 'ten (10) business days', 'registered or certified mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid' with 'mailing via standard post, return receipt unnecessary, postage collect', 'dispatched by electronic facsimile transmission' with 'sending via digital fax', 'internationally recognized overnight courier service' with 'nationally known express courier', 'Party' with 'Entity'. The change in terminology and conditions (altering delivery times, postage responsibility, and courier types) introduces ambiguity regarding the notification process, making it challenging to ascertain whether notice was validly served. This contradicts the clause's original intention of ensuring clarity and reliability in providing notices. This is contradicting with section 19 Binding effect clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 18. Binding Effect All of the terms and provisions of this Agreement by or for the benefit of the Parties shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of their successors, assigns, heirs and personal representatives. The rights and obligations provided by this Agreement shall not be assignable by any Party. Except as expressly provided herein, nothing herein is intended to confer upon any person, other than the Parties and their successors, any rights or remedies under or by reason of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change in terminology and specific conditions regarding notice delivery (altering delivery times, postage responsibility, and courier types) does not directly contradict the Binding Effect clause. The Binding Effect clause pertains to the agreement's enforceability and transferability to successors, assigns, heirs, and personal representatives. While the changes to the notification process may impact the practical application of the agreement, they do not introduce a fundamental conflict with who is bound by the agreement's terms. Therefore, it does not meet the threshold for a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties shall hereafter each take those actions and execute and deliver those documents and instruments as shall be reasonably necessary in order to fulfill the intent and purpose of this Agreement, and shall cooperate in any filing, registration, investigation or other activity that shall be required or shall occur as a result of or in connection with this transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: The Collaborators shall thereafter individually undertake such steps and prepare and furnish such papers and tools as may be deemed suitably advisable to realize the spirit and essence of this Accord, and will participate in any submission, listing, inquiry or additional undertaking that may be requisite or arise as a consequence of or pertaining to this interaction.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'Parties' is being replaced with 'Collaborators', 'actions' with 'steps', 'documents and instruments' with 'papers and tools', 'reasonably necessary' with 'suitably advisable', 'intent and purpose' with 'spirit and essence', 'Agreement' with 'Accord', 'cooperate' with 'participate', 'filing, registration, investigation or other activity' with 'submission, listing, inquiry or additional undertaking' and 'transaction' with 'interaction'. By changing the terminology from clear directives (like 'shall take those actions' and 'shall cooperate') to more ambiguous terms ('undertake such steps' and 'will participate'), the strength of the obligation is diminished. This shift can lead to uncertainty regarding the extent of each party's responsibilities and commitment to the agreement's goals. Further, altering 'reasonably necessary' to 'suitably advisable' grants more discretion to the parties, potentially leading to subjective interpretations of what actions are required. This is contradicting with section 7 that define obligations and warrants.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Representations, Warranties, And Covenants Of GINARES GINARES hereby represents, warrants, and covenants to HPIL ET that: 7.1. Authorization This Agreement constitutes a valid and legally binding obligation of GINARES, enforceable in accordance with its terms, except (i) as limited by applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganization, moratorium and other laws of general application affecting enforcement of creditors' rights generally and (ii) as limited by laws relating to the availability of specific performance, injunctive relief or other equitable remedies. 7.2. Consents To GINARES's knowledge, no consent, approval, order or authorization of, or registration, qualification, designation, declaration or filing with, any federal, state or local governmental authority on the part of GINARES is required in connection with the consummation of the transactions contemplated by this Agreement. 7.3. Compliance With Other Instruments The execution, delivery and performance of this Agreement contemplated hereby will not result in a violation of, or default under, any instrument, judgment, order, writ, decree or contract known to GINARES, or an event that results in the creation of any lien, charge or encumbrance upon the Agreement. 7.4. Litigation There is no action, suit, proceeding or investigation pending or, to GINARES's knowledge, currently threatened that questions the validity of this Agreement, or the right of GINARES to enter into this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the obligations and commitments of the parties by replacing terms that create clear directives with more ambiguous ones. This introduces a contradiction with Section 7, which establishes representations, warranties, and covenants that are meant to be valid and legally binding obligations. The shift towards subjective interpretations of required actions and diminished obligations undermines the enforceability outlined in Section 7.1, thus creating a strong contradiction.
